reviews.review_description:
game is decent until the race level, your supposed to fly across these gaps but unfortunately most the time you just fall and die. Game needed more playtesting

Im not saying it overshoot the gap. Im saying the vehicle never switches to flight mode so I just fall. This is not fun, I won't be changing my review sorry

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! The race level is definitely a bit tricky at first, but it becomes very fun once you get the hang of it. The key is to control the accelerator and not keep it fully pressed all the time, otherwise you’ll overshoot the gaps.

If that specific level (the only one like this) isn’t your thing, you can also switch to Easy difficulty to get past it more quickly. If this helps improve your experience, we’d really appreciate it if you could update the review, as it genuinely helps us keep improving the game.

reviews.review_description: Usually all games from Ivanovich are impressive. But this feels like the immersive parts of 1976, without a main game. The sound samples are from other games. It feels a bit like a techdemo. Some 3d models from old ivanovich games. To be honest, it feels like recycling. Some levels are introduced for the future. What this means, i already know from the last game inRun. The introduced fresh levels are never been released. I am dominating the inRun list and waiting for more levels. Compared to all other ivanovich games, this game is poor. Cant recommend it for 6 bucks. For free it would be okayish, but nothing i will play frequently or a second time.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your feedback. Just to clarify: Chapter 1 of Echo Wars is already complete with 8 levels, each one with brand new enemies and bosses created exclusively for this game. Chapter 2 will add another 8 levels, published weekly until the full 16 are ready during Early Access.

We always recycle part of our work to create new games, but Echo Wars is not just a tech demo — every level brings fresh challenges, enemies and set-pieces. And if you enjoy replayability, going for triple gold medals in the hardest difficulty is a near-impossible challenge that adds tons of value.

We hope you’ll give it another chance when more levels are out and see how the full experience comes together.

reviews.review_description: Nice to finally see something different, gameplay seemed good, steering with motion controls wasn't great but was usable. Gun play was good.

Great fun driving around shooting monsters and armed vehicles what's not to like? You can even fly lol!

This is NOT a tech demo it's a fun driving flying arcade shooter!
It honestly has great gameplay with different levels of difficulty and the graphics are great too.

 I'm  left handed and I'm grateful to the developers for including the left hand option- it feels like a very polished game even though its in early access.

For the price, it's a 'no brainer' just buy it! It's supposed to have the arcade feel, when you finish the tutorial you enter an arcade environment and you have the option to check out the other developers games all in arcade machines...good job developers 
Five stars from me
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
